name: Valdemurio Reservoir Bear's Path Loop
description: Enjoy an easy 2.0-mile (3.3 km) circular jogging route around Embalse de Valdemurio, offering scenic views of the reservoir and mountains.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Quirós traverse a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes, including high peaks and deep gorges. The region features a mix of challenging mountain trails and more accessible paths around reservoirs and through valleys. Runners can expect varied terrain, from rugged ascents to gentler, scenic loops. Best jogging routes around Quirós The most popular jogging route is Valdemurio Reservoir Bear's Path Loop, an easy 2.0 miles (3.3 km) trail that takes about 23 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is Ricabo to Alto de Ventana Trail — circular via the GR 207, a difficult 10.4 miles (16.7 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gain and panoramic views from Alto de Ventana. Local runners also love the Ascent to the Peñas Ubinas — circular from Tuíza, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant climbs, often completed in about 3 hours 46 minutes. Jogging around Quirós is defined by mountainous ascents, reservoir-side paths, and deep gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to difficult mountain challenges. The routes in Quirós are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Quirós's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Quirós?
A: Quirós offers a diverse selection of nearly 40 running routes. These range from easy loops to challenging mountain trails, catering to various fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Quirós?
A: Jogging routes in Quirós feature a varied landscape, from paths around reservoirs and through valleys to challenging mountain trails with significant elevation changes. You'll encounter rugged ascents, gentle scenic loops, and deep gorges.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Quirós?
A: Yes, Quirós has a few easy jogging routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run. One excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240532833/valdemurio-reservoir-bear-s-path-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdemurio Reservoir Bear's Path Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 3.3 km trail offering pleasant views.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Quirós?
A: Many of the running routes in Quirós are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For a more challenging circular option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240516498/ricabo-to-alto-de-ventana-trail-circular-via-the-gr-207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricabo to Alto de Ventana Trail — circular via the GR 207&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views.

Q: What are some scenic jogging routes with viewpoints in Quirós?
A: For breathtaking views, the region's mountainous routes are ideal. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240516498/ricabo-to-alto-de-ventana-trail-circular-via-the-gr-207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricabo to Alto de Ventana Trail&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic vistas from Alto de Ventana. Additionally, routes leading to passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6333018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cubilla Pass&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/176266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventana Pass&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning mountain scenery.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running trails in Quirós?
A: While many routes in Quirós involve significant elevation, the easier trails around the Valdemurio Reservoir, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240532833/valdemurio-reservoir-bear-s-path-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdemurio Reservoir Bear's Path Loop&lt;/a&gt;, are generally suitable for families looking for a less strenuous run or walk.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging trails in Quirós?
A: Many trails in Quirós are dog-friendly, especially those in natural areas. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6736914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt; (Bear Trail) is a popular option for runners with dogs.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural attractions can I see along the running routes?
A: Quirós is rich in natural beauty. Along your runs, you might encounter impressive gorges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/297709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Xanas&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2949141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Peñas Juntas&lt;/a&gt;. The famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5725922" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bear Trail&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable attraction in the vicinity.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Quirós?
A: The best time for jogging in Quirós is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or evenings, though some higher mountain routes might be very warm. Winter running is possible but requires appropriate gear due to potential snow and colder conditions, particularly at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by cafes or places to rest?
A: While many routes are in natural, remote areas, some trails, especially those closer to villages or popular attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6736914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt;, may have cafes or small eateries nearby. It's recommended to check the route details or local information for specific amenities along your chosen path.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Quirós?
A: The running routes in Quirós are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the stunning mountain scenery, the variety of terrain, and the sense of adventure offered by the challenging ascents and remote paths.

Q: How long are the typical jogging trails in Quirós?
A: The length of jogging trails in Quirós varies significantly. You can find shorter, easy loops like the 3.3 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240532833/valdemurio-reservoir-bear-s-path-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdemurio Reservoir Bear's Path Loop&lt;/a&gt;, as well as much longer and more challenging routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158917" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de los Pícaros – Peñas Juntas Gorge loop from Proaza&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 30 km.
tips: The Valdemurio Reservoir Footbridge is a structure that allows visitors to enjoy panoramic views of the reservoir and its natural surroundings. This footbridge is part of the Senda del Oso, a very popular hiking and cycling route in Asturias.

The Valdemurio Reservoir is located in the municipality of Quirós, in Asturias, and is an ideal place to enjoy nature and do various outdoor activities. This reservoir is part of the Vía Verde de la Senda del Oso, a very popular route for hiking and cycling.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Embalse de Valdemurio circular route?
faqs.answer: This easy circular route is designed for jogging and typically takes around 23-24 minutes to complete. Its gentle gradient and well-maintained paths make it a quick and enjoyable run.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Valdemurio circular trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely! This trail is considered an "easy run" with minimal elevation changes, making it perfect for beginners, casual joggers, and families. It's also a popular choice for those with pets.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and generally easy. You'll encounter about 1.67 km of gravel, 819 meters of unpaved sections, and 479 meters of paved areas. This mix provides a comfortable surface for jogging without being overly challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Embalse de Valdemurio circular trail?
faqs.answer: There is free parking available near the trailhead, making it convenient to access the route. Look for designated parking areas close to the reservoir.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy beautiful panoramic views of the Valdemurio Reservoir and its natural surroundings. A notable highlight is the Valdemurio Footbridge, which offers stunning vistas across the water and landscape.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see around the Valdemurio Reservoir?
faqs.answer: The area boasts a rich ecosystem. You might spot aquatic birds, various raptors, deer, and foxes. There's even a chance of seeing the elusive Cantabrian brown bear, as the region is part of their habitat.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Valdemurio circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is very dog-friendly! It's a popular spot for locals to walk their pets. Just remember to keep your dog on a leash and clean up after them to preserve the natural beauty for everyone.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early morning or late afternoon jog is recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to jog the Embalse de Valdemurio circular trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and jog this circular trail around the Valdemurio Reservoir.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route utilizes a section of the renowned Senda del Oso (Bear's Path), a popular hiking and cycling route in Asturias. It also overlaps with parts of the Asturias Interior (Etapa 15) and Sendero Histórico: Asturias trails.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area around the reservoir is well-equipped for visitors. You'll find picnic areas, and there's often a bar nearby for refreshments. The reservoir also offers facilities for water sports like canoeing and paddleboarding.
